page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU architecture is arm64, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory write operations and single-threaded tasks. The Passmark memory score ranks in the top tier, supported by a strong memory bandwidth write speed of 86.22 GB/sec for large blocks. Single-threaded performance is also notable, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores, as well as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds, placing in the strong tier. Conversely, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the weak tier. Multi-core performance, static web serving, and database operations consistently register in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gn.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, leveraging the power-efficient ARMv9 architecture to deliver competitive benchmark-per-resource ratios. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, text processing, and light database operations. It can also handle small-scale language model inference, though prompt processing for medium-sized models shows poor performance, indicating a limitation for heavier AI workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this arm64 server includes 32.0 GB of RAM, a 36 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with a strong Passmark memory score and a large-block write speed of 86.22 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like text processing and gzip compression also perform strongly. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the weak tier on Passmark encryption tests. The instance delivers average-tier results for multi-core processing, database operations, and static web serving. It is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and web serving, offering a balanced resource profile without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 8 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose server featuring dedicated ARMv9 cores, top-tier memory performance, and high-speed network optimization without local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 8 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory performance with 86.22 GB/sec large-block write bandwidth
Strong single-threaded execution in text processing and compression
Tradeoff in cryptographic performance, scoring in the lower tier
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
